name: Creation or Modification of a new GPO Scheduled Task or Service
notes:
This rule detects the creation or modification of a new Group Policy based scheduled task or service.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Samir Bousseaden
SIGMA Title: Persistence and Execution at Scale via GPO Scheduled Task
SIGMA Description: Detect lateral movement using GPO scheduled task, usually used to deploy ransomware at scale


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001830) FileCreate

when the event matches LOWER("Target File Directory") MATCHES '(.*\\windows\\sysvol\\domain\\policies\\.*\\machine\\preferences\\scheduledtasks\\scheduledtasks\.xml$|.*\\windows\\sysvol\\domain\\policies\\.*\\machine\\preferences\\services\\services\.xml$)' AQL filter query
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of dfsrs.exe
TD_list: APPLY Creation or Modification of a new GPO Scheduled Task or Service on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001830) FileCreate

AND when the event matches LOWER("Target File Directory") MATCHES '(.*\\windows\\sysvol\\domain\\policies\\.*\\machine\\preferences\\scheduledtasks\\scheduledtasks\.xml$|.*\\windows\\sysvol\\domain\\policies\\.*\\machine\\preferences\\services\\services\.xml$)' AQL filter query
AND NOT when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of dfsrs.exe
RED_list: Event name: Creation or Modification of a new GPO Scheduled Task or Service
Event description: Detects the creation or modification of a new Group Policy based scheduled task or service.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
